name: Blue Barn Park
description: Blue Barn Park is a place to play pickleball in Allentown, PA. There are 3 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like restrooms.

name: Furnace Dam Park
description: Furnace Dam Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Emmaus, PA. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Grange Road Park
description: Grange Road Park is a place to play pickleball in Allentown, PA. There are 12 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Amenities include restrooms, water, and wheelchair accessibility.

name: Green Acres Park
description: Green Acres Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Allentown, PA. There are 4 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Hopewell park
description: Public park courts, small fence surrounding, lots of parking

name: Indepenence Park
description: Indepenence Park is a place to play pickleball in Schnecksville, PA. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Jewish Community Center Of The Lehigh Valley
description: Jewish Community Center Of The Lehigh Valley is a place to play pickleball in Allentown, PA. There is 1 indoor acrylic court. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and water.

name: Laurys Firehouse Park
description: Laurys Firehouse Park is a place to play pickleball in Laurys Station, PA. There are 3 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to restrooms. You can’t reserve the courts. The courts are free.

name: Lehigh Country Club
description: Come play pickleball at Lehigh Country Club in Allentown, PA! There are 8 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A membership is required to play. Players have access to restrooms and lights. Reservations are available.

name: Lehigh Valley Active Life
description: Lehigh Valley Active Life is a place to play pickleball in Allentown, PA. There are 2 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Lehigh Valley Tennis Club
description: Come play pickleball at Lehigh Valley Tennis Club in Allentown, PA! There are 2 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts can be reserved. A membership is required to play. Players have access to restrooms.

name: Lindberg Park
description: Lindberg Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Allentown, PA. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and water.

name: Lower Macungie Municipal Park
description: Lower Macungie Municipal Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Macungie, PA. There are 8 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. You can’t reserve the courts. The facility includes amenities like lights, restrooms, and wheelchair accessibility.

name: Pickleball Kingdom Allentown
description: COMING SOON!

name: Pickleball Kingdom - Lehigh Valley
description: Pickleball Kingdom - Lehigh Valley is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Fullerton, PA. There are 14 indoor courts.

name: Pickleball Lehigh valley
description: Come play pickleball at Pickleball Lehigh valley in Allentown, PA! There are 34 wood and asphalt courts. 22 are outdoors and 12 are indoors. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include restrooms, a pro shop or equipment store, wheelchair accessibility, lights, and trainers or lessons. A one-time fee is required to play. Reservations are available.

name: Prater Park
description: There is one dedicated pickleball court. Two lined tennis courts with tennis nets.

name: Rodale County Park
description: Rodale County Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Allentown, PA. There is 1 outdoor hard court. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The court is free.

name: Slatington Social
description: Come play pickleball at Slatington Social in Slatington, PA! There are 4 indoor acrylic and hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. You can reserve the courts. The courts are free. Players have access to food, restrooms, youth programming, lights, trainers or lessons, wheelchair accessibility, and water.

name: Wild Cherry Park
description: Park gets very busy during peak youth soccer and softball seasons.
